title: Control consolidation: activate D-002, reconcile stale PRs, first-wave contract + packets
objective: Capture D-002; reconcile PRs #93/#94/#95/#96/#91/#92/#64; port only currently-valid, independently-supported control changes; record LOW-1; build the first-wave integration contract and contract the first-wave producer packets (M3-T001, M4-T007, M2-T017); refresh exactly one handoff. Control-plane only: no product code, no shared-hotspot edits, no producer dispatch.
business_reason: Owner directive D-002 sections 2-7: one fresh control-consolidation from post-#97 main before the first blind-isolated parallel build wave.
outputs: project-control/tasks/M3-T001.json, M4-T007.json, M2-T017.json (first-wave producer packets, in-regime, citing D-002)
project-control/tasks/M3-T002.json, M3-T003.json, M3-T004.json, M3-T005.json (ported M3 chain, backlog, sequential)
project-control/tasks/M0-T019.json (frontend-security target + eligibility reconciliation ported from #94)
project-control/master_plan.json (M3 chain reflected; no scope change without owner)
docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md (single refreshed handoff on consolidated main)
forbidden_paths: tools/** EXCEPT tools/test_directive_compliance.py (controller-only test-maintenance: the CI-exposed D-002-squat fixture fix, D-002 Section 6 reserves control tests for the controller; the directive/control CLI, validators, and other control tests remain frozen)
any product/runtime code; any first-wave producer's allowed paths or outputs
acceptance_scenarios: AS-1 (executable): python tools/validate_directive_compliance.py --check exits 0; D-002 is active with 69 locked requirements and matching id/content digests.
AS-2 (executable): python tools/project_control.py status shows M0-T024, M3-T001, M4-T007, M2-T017 present, in-regime, citing D-002; ref coverage OK for each (evaluate_task_refs ok).

AS-3: project-control/reports/FIRST-WAVE-INTEGRATION-CONTRACT.md freezes every D-002 Section-5 field (task id, base SHA, deps, outputs, allowed/forbidden paths, owned+consumed interfaces + schema versions, generated-artifact sole owner, DB/migration/env/port ownership, harness responsibilities, report path, acceptance evidence, stop conditions, downstream integration task) for each first-wave producer, and includes a semantic-collision matrix proving disjoint ownership + no unmerged-sibling dependency.

AS-4: LOW-1 recorded durably as a narrowly scoped control-plane backlog item (path containment for trusted checked-in requirements_file/verification_file references), stating fail-closed behavior is preserved and it is NOT implemented in this directive.

AS-5: project-control/reports/M0-T024-PR-DISPOSITIONS.md records, for each of #93/#94/#95/#96/#91/#92/#64, whether each unique material change was carried forward / superseded (with reason) / still unresolved.
AS-6: exactly one docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md reflects consolidated main; no contradictory handoffs introduced.

AS-7: The preserved items of D-002 Section 4 are all present (M3 5-task chain + Document Evidence Verification Engine; whole-system A-J analysis; decimal-arithmetic publication blocker B-014; frontend security target + age rule + age-exception-declined decision; M0-T019 eligibility 2026-07-28T15:59:32.231Z; all holds G6/legal/credentials/deployment/survey/paid).
risks: Porting stale-PR content could reintroduce superseded claims - mitigated by per-PR disposition ledger + independent control-plane review.
Over-binding via directive applicability - mitigated by scoped CTRL/PROD/POST groups (no wildcard requirements).

progress_log.message: G4 control-plane FAIL (independent): B-011 detail contained bare tokens M3-T001 + M3-T004, so open B-011 spuriously blocked accept() of first-wave M3-T001 and coupled M3-T004 via _blocker_references. Rework: reword B-011 to keep only M3-T005 matchable; fix test_directive_compliance.py D-002-squat (CI control-plane FAIL); add superseding banner to ported M3-CORPUS proposal (G3 advisory).
